header.subtitle: `.agents/` Structure + `AGENT.md`
nav.label: Documentation map
nav.desc: A short tree for routing reading. Enter through the right category, not through the entire tree.
sec.overview.h2: A scalable agent system starts with short routers
sec.overview.intro: The foundation is not a giant prompt. The foundation is a small set of routers, organized memory, modular skills, explicit tasks, and YAML indexes that allow cheap reading. AGENT.md is the entry point, not the whole memory.
sec.overview.rule.label: Mandatory rule
sec.overview.rule.text: Without consistent frontmatter, there is no organizable memory. Without YAML indexes, there is no cheap routing.
sec.overview.good.label: Correct AGENT.md
sec.overview.good.li1: Short, stable, and cheap in tokens
sec.overview.good.li2: Points to indexes before detailed files
sec.overview.good.li3: Separates durable memory from work in progress
sec.overview.good.li4: Loads only what the task requires
sec.overview.bad.label: Bad AGENT.md
sec.overview.bad.li1: Tries to contain the whole truth of the project
sec.overview.bad.li2: Mixes rules, decisions, tickets, and historical context
sec.overview.bad.li3: Forces long reading before any action
sec.overview.bad.li4: Becomes a dumping ground nobody can maintain
sec.overview.example.title: Lightweight bootstrap example
sec.overview.example.desc: Short opening, links to routers, and explicit instruction not to read everything.
sec.overview.example.code:
# AGENT.md

This file is the lightweight boot context for agents.
Use it as a routing map, not as the full project memory.

- Ticket work: read .agents/tasks/index.yaml first
- Durable memory router: read .agents/memory/MEMORY.md
- Architecture context: read .agents/memory/architecture/index.yaml first
- Project principles: read .agents/memory/principles/index.yaml first

Only load files relevant to the current task.
sec.layout.h2: The `.agents/` anatomy is operational, not decorative
sec.layout.intro: The tree below is a generic starting point. It organizes context for discovery, selective reading, and area-based evolution. It is not dogma, but the minimum pillars stay the same.
sec.layout.tree.code:
.agents/
├─ AGENT.md
├─ memory/
│  ├─ MEMORY.md
│  ├─ architecture/
│  │  ├─ index.yaml
│  │  └─ overview.md
│  ├─ decisions/
│  │  ├─ index.yaml
│  │  └─ ADR-001-example.md
│  └─ principles/
│     ├─ index.yaml
│     └─ constitution.md
├─ skills/
│  ├─ code-review/
│  │  ├─ SKILL.md
│  │  └─ agents/openai.yaml
│  └─ planning/
│     └─ SKILL.md
├─ tasks/
│  ├─ index.yaml
│  ├─ indexes/
│  │  └─ TKT-001-025.yaml
│  └─ TKT-001/
│     ├─ task.md
│     ├─ plan.md
│     └─ report.md
└─ tools/
   └─ sync_indexes.py
sec.layout.card.router.title: Routers
sec.layout.card.router.desc: AGENT.md and MEMORY.md guide the first read. They exist to reduce context, not to replace it.
sec.layout.card.pillars.title: Minimum pillars
sec.layout.card.pillars.desc: Skills store reusable procedure. Memory stores durable knowledge. Tasks store operational history per unit of work.
sec.skills.intro: A skill should capture a recurring workflow with narrow scope and clear action. It avoids repeating long instructions in every prompt and turns team patterns into reusable execution.
sec.skills.card.planning.title: `planning/`
sec.skills.card.planning.desc: Creates or updates execution plans and checkpoints.
sec.skills.card.review.title: `code-review/`
sec.skills.card.review.desc: Standardizes review with explicit criteria and consistent output.
sec.skills.card.reflect.title: `reflect/`
sec.skills.card.reflect.desc: Captures lessons at the end of a session and decides what becomes durable memory.
sec.skills.warning.label: Point that must stay explicit
sec.skills.warning.text: Skill does not replace memory. Skill defines procedure.
sec.skills.example.title: Small and actionable structure
sec.skills.example.code:
.agents/skills/code-review/
├─ SKILL.md
└─ agents/
   └─ openai.yaml

# SKILL.md
Review the target diff systematically.

- Check correctness first
- Then regressions
- Then missing tests
- Return findings ordered by severity
sec.memory.h2: Memory is durable and routable knowledge
sec.memory.intro: AGENT.md does global routing. MEMORY.md routes memory. After that come small thematic subfolders with index.yaml and focused Markdown files.
sec.memory.rules.title: Memory rules
sec.memory.rules.li1: Every important file starts with YAML frontmatter.
sec.memory.rules.li2: Each file covers a clear, reusable topic.
sec.memory.rules.li3: Read the index first, then the relevant file.
sec.memory.rules.li4: index.yaml helps discovery, not canonical truth.
sec.memory.router.title: Reading flow
sec.memory.router.desc: `AGENT.md` → `MEMORY.md` → thematic index → correct Markdown file. This chain preserves context and stops the agent from scanning the whole tree unnecessarily.
sec.memory.fm.title: Memory frontmatter example
sec.memory.index.title: Thematic index example
sec.memory.fm.code:
---
id: architecture-overview
title: Architecture overview
type: architecture
status: active
created: 2026-05-20
updated: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - architecture
  - boundaries
read_when:
  - changing app boundaries
  - deciding folder structure
---

# Architecture overview
sec.memory.index.code:
entries:
  - id: architecture-overview
    title: Architecture overview
    file: .agents/memory/architecture/overview.md
    tags:
      - architecture
      - boundaries
    read_when:
      - changing app boundaries
      - deciding folder structure

  - id: ADR-001
    title: Agent ticket lifecycle
    file: .agents/memory/decisions/ADR-001-agent-ticket-lifecycle.md
    tags:
      - adr
      - tasks
sec.tasks.h2: Tasks store operational history per unit of work
sec.tasks.intro: One folder per ticket, issue, or relevant work item. The goal is to keep the narrative of that change in one place, with minimal artifacts for resuming context, auditing decisions, and enabling continuity.
sec.tasks.card.truth.title: Source of truth
sec.tasks.card.truth.desc: The `TKT-XXX/` folder is the real history of the work. `task.md` is the minimum artifact. `plan.md`, `report.md`, `concept.md`, and `updates/` enter only when scope justifies them.
sec.tasks.card.index.title: Index role
sec.tasks.card.index.desc: `tasks/index.yaml` and shards are navigation and discovery shortcuts. They do not replace the files inside the task folder.
sec.tasks.fm.title: Task frontmatter example
sec.tasks.fm.code:
---
id: TKT-012
title: Module federation shell host
artifact: task
status: done
owner: codex
created: 2026-05-25
updated: 2026-05-25
dependencies:
  - TKT-011
areas:
  - apps/ui
  - apps/tauri
skills:
  - ui-implementation
  - tkt-management
---

# TKT-012 Task
sec.indexes.h2: YAML indexing is the mechanism that avoids opening the entire tree
sec.indexes.intro: Without indexes, the agent tends to open full directories and pay a high context cost. With small indexes and range-based shards, reading is routed by topic, interval, or artifact before touching detailed content.
sec.indexes.rule.label: Recommended rule
sec.indexes.rule.text: If a file is indexable, it must be synchronizable.
sec.indexes.memory.title: Memory index by category
sec.indexes.tasks.title: Task shard by range
sec.indexes.memory.code:
entries:
  - id: principles-constitution
    file: .agents/memory/principles/constitution.md
    status: active
  - id: architecture-overview
    file: .agents/memory/architecture/overview.md
    status: active
sec.indexes.tasks.code:
- range: TKT-001-025
  file: .agents/tasks/indexes/TKT-001-025.yaml
  count: 12
  updated: 2026-05-25
  statuses:
    - blocked: 1
    - done: 8
    - planning: 3
sec.indexes.card.schema.title: Stable schema
sec.indexes.card.schema.desc: Define consistent YAML fields for memory, tasks, and indexes.
sec.indexes.card.sync.title: Sync and validation
sec.indexes.card.sync.desc: Keep a script to regenerate and validate indexes whenever artifacts change.
sec.indexes.card.discovery.title: Cheap discovery
sec.indexes.card.discovery.desc: Read the smallest useful file first. That is what the index is for.
sec.anti.h2: Anti-patterns appear when everything becomes loose text
sec.anti.intro: If the system does not distinguish router, memory, task, and skill, it loses scale quickly. These are the errors that most degrade clarity and context cost.
sec.anti.card.1.title: Using `AGENT.md` as a dumping ground
sec.anti.card.1.desc: When everything goes into the entry file, it stops routing and starts blocking.
sec.anti.card.2.title: Creating `.md` files without YAML frontmatter
sec.anti.card.2.desc: Without stable metadata, you cannot index, sync, or route safely.
sec.anti.card.3.title: Not separating durable memory from temporary task context
sec.anti.card.3.desc: Stable knowledge and operational history have different lifecycles. Mixing them weakens both.
sec.anti.card.4.title: Duplicating knowledge in `skills` and `memory`
sec.anti.card.4.desc: Skill should point to procedure. Memory should hold reusable knowledge.
sec.anti.card.5.title: Keeping `index.yaml` manual and stale
sec.anti.card.5.desc: If the index does not track the tree, it becomes noise instead of guidance.
sec.adaptacao.h2: The pattern is generic, the depth changes with the project
sec.adaptacao.intro: The structure does not depend on a single stack. What changes is the level of detail, not the distinction between router, memory, skills, tasks, and indexes.
sec.adaptacao.card.1.label: Solo project
sec.adaptacao.card.1.title: Lean minimum
sec.adaptacao.card.1.desc: `AGENT.md`, `memory/`, one or two skills, and a simple `tasks/index.yaml` already go a long way.
sec.adaptacao.card.2.label: SaaS product
sec.adaptacao.card.2.title: Domain-based memory
sec.adaptacao.card.2.desc: Architecture, decisions, billing, auth, onboarding, and operations often justify separate thematic indexes.
sec.adaptacao.card.3.label: Monorepo
sec.adaptacao.card.3.title: Shards and areas
sec.adaptacao.card.3.desc: Range-sharded tasks and area metadata help route reading across apps, libs, and tools.
sec.starter.h2: Starter kit for standing up the structure without excess
sec.starter.intro: If you want to start from zero, start small and standardized. The structure can grow later without losing clarity.
sec.starter.step1.title: Create `AGENT.md`
sec.starter.step1.desc: Make it a lightweight bootstrap with links to memory, skills, and tasks.
sec.starter.step2.title: Create `.agents/memory/MEMORY.md`
sec.starter.step2.desc: Use this file as the durable memory router.
sec.starter.step3.title: Create the first skill
sec.starter.step3.desc: Pick a repetitive flow and turn it into reusable procedure.
sec.starter.step4.title: Create `tasks/index.yaml`
sec.starter.step4.desc: Even a small index already enforces good discovery discipline.
sec.starter.step5.title: Standardize frontmatter in every routable `.md`
sec.starter.step5.desc: If a file needs to be found by an AI, it needs to be indexable.
sec.starter.step6.title: Automate index sync
sec.starter.step6.desc: Do not let critical metadata depend on manual updates.
sec.starter.end.label: Final summary
sec.starter.end.text: `AGENT.md` routes entry. `memory/` stores durable context. `skills/` stores procedure. `tasks/` stores operational history. `index.yaml` keeps everything readable without blowing the context budget.
